page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 24 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance.

In benchmark testing, the m5zn.12xlarge exhibits strong memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 232.82 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 97.66 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.93 GB/sec. CPU-bound benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core, fall into the average tier. LLM text generation performance is weak, with the 7B model achieving 7.75 tokens/sec, indicating that this CPU-only instance is not optimized for heavy generative AI inference.

The instance provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-intensive applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5zn.12xlarge offers a premium hardware profile due to its high-frequency Intel Xeon cores and high-speed networking, positioning it as a specialized resource-dense option compared to standard general-purpose instances. It is best suited for workloads requiring high single-core performance and massive network throughput, such as high-performance web serving, database nodes, gaming servers, and financial simulation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a high-performance, general-purpose instance optimized for network and EBS throughput. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a substantial 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong memory bandwidth and compression speeds, alongside average CPU performance. Qualitatively, its high-frequency cores and high network capacity provide excellent resource density for demanding applications. It is highly suited for network-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-performance web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-performance and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, network-optimized virtual server delivering balanced memory and compute resources with a 100 Gbps baseline network interface.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads
Strong memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
EBS-only storage configuration with no local NVMe or GPU hardware
Best suited for high-performance web serving and database operations
